speaker: Innkeeper Bran
text: Beds are clean, tea is questionable. You staying?

speaker: Player
text: Just passing through.

speaker: Innkeeper Bran
text: Then pass through warm. Wind bites at night.

speaker: Caretaker Iva
text: Mind the chalk lines—they’re a fierce tournament.

speaker: Player
text: What’s the prize?

speaker: Caretaker Iva
text: Bragging. Best currency in the yard.

speaker: Juno
text: Courier on a deadline—talk fast or walk with me.

speaker: Player
text: Any news?

speaker: Juno
text: Packages go missing near the docks. Someone’s skimming. Keep your eyes open.

speaker: Sergeant Leto
text: Papers, please. Ha—kidding. We’re short on papers today.

speaker: Player
text: Anything we should avoid?

speaker: Sergeant Leto
text: Back alley after midnight. Unless you like buying trouble twice.

speaker: Pasha
text: Step right up—today's lots are honest and only mildly cursed!

speaker: Player
text: We're just looking.

speaker: Pasha
text: Looking is free. Advice, too: bids rise when the drones clock off—late evenings are fierce.

speaker: Rana
text: Watch your step. Half these pallets wobble worse than a drunk loader.

speaker: Player
text: Busy day?

speaker: Rana
text: Always. If a crate's tagged in blue, it’s bound for the mine. Don’t touch blue.

speaker: Sedge
text: You look like someone who can mind a secret.

speaker: Player
text: Depends on the secret.

speaker: Sedge
text: Then it depends on the price. Come back when you’ve got something to trade.

speaker: Doctor Sera
text: If you’re bleeding, sit. If you’re not, don’t start.

speaker: Player
text: We’re fine.

speaker: Doctor Sera
text: Good. I’m running low on synth-gel and patience in equal measure.

speaker: Sima
text: Noodles hot, pepper hotter. Want steam or fire?

speaker: Player
text: Surprise me.

speaker: Sima
text: Bold. I like bold. Here—don’t breathe in too deep.

speaker: Tallo
text: New faces? Welcome. Roof leaks, hearts don’t.

speaker: Player
text: Nice place.

speaker: Tallo
text: It’s home. The trick is to keep it ours.

speaker: Ellie
text: Just swapping out panels before the sparks get ambitious. What do you need?

speaker: Ellie
text: The arcade's alive again! Here, a little something for keeping the lights bright.

speaker: Jed
text: Nova! Thank the currents. I heard the alarm broadcast. The Warden's security is descending, sealing everything.

speaker: Nova
text: Jed, they're calling me a contaminant. Zeke's overriding the gates, but we have to move now. Come on!

speaker: Jed
text: I can't, girl. The manual override panel for this lift is failing. If I let go of this handle, the lift locks down, and they pin you in this shaft.

speaker: Nova
text: No! We can jam the gears! I'm not leaving you behind!

speaker: Jed
text: This isn't a quota run, Nova. You have to launch. Here—take this Chime. It was in the generator core housing. It's the only thing that will bypass the launch bay locks. Now run, and don't look back.

speaker: Jed
text: You look like a pressure leak with boots. Tell me you slept.

speaker: Nova
text: I slept enough. Quota doesn't care how rested I am.

speaker: Jed
text: Quota also doesn't care if that cutter pops and takes your hand with it. I do.

speaker: Nova
text: If I don't push it, I miss count. If I miss count, I lose scrip. If I lose scrip...

speaker: Jed
text: I know the math. I hate the math. Check your gear, then use the bench. And take the ration bar before you pretend you aren't hungry.

speaker: Jed
text: Grab a seat if you're sticking around. If something starts smoking, hand me the wrench first.

speaker: Jed
text: You made it back in one piece. That surge settle down once you pulled the core?

speaker: Jed
text: Then we learn what they wanted. I'll crack the housing and see how far the signal goes. Nice work bringing it home.

speaker: Jed
text: Town runs smoother thanks to you. Keep pace like that and we'll stay ahead of the scrappers.

speaker: Jed
text: Stellarium core is cooling on my workbench thanks to you. We'll make good use of it.

speaker: Jed
text: Smell that ozone? That's the core spool. Hand it over before it cooks through your belt.

speaker: Jed
text: Track that pulse to its source. If the deep mine lit up, something below Sector 4 is chewing through power.

speaker: Jed
text: Nice work. That's all of 'em. Now, get this gear back to their owners. They've been pestering me for days.

speaker: Schoolteacher Kasey
text: Quiet feet, please. The little ones are mapping constellations they have never been allowed to see.

speaker: Schoolteacher Kasey
text: Wonderful! The kids will be back under the stars tonight. Please, take this for your trouble.

speaker: Maddie
text: Beans are roasting, and for once the grinder is not threatening to explode. Did you need something?

speaker: Maddie
text: You fixed it! Oh, thank you! Take these credits - may your next cup be on me.

speaker: Nova
text: Burrower was feeding on it. Nest is quiet now, but whoever rigged the chamber knew what they were doing.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Badge.

speaker: Nova
text: Morning to you too.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Morning costs extra. Badge is mandatory. Booth window, then maybe the door likes you.

speaker: Guard Hank
text: Keep the line moving. The door doesn't care about your reasons.

speaker: Zeke
text: Name? ID? Clearance sco--oh. It's you. The volunteer.

speaker: Nova
text: I need a mining pass, suit. Shift starts in ten.

speaker: Zeke
text: Right. Eligibility check. Completely routine. Very normal.

speaker: Zeke
text: ...Mandatory Retirement. Asset Liability exceeds projected value. Recycle.

speaker: Nova
text: Recycle means fired, right?

speaker: Zeke
text: It means bio-reclamation. Death. For a breaker surge.

speaker: Zeke
text: I'm flagging it as grid instability. Temporary override. Take the badge before I remember how rules work.

speaker: Zeke
text: The system logged something. I should not have said that out loud.

speaker: Scrapper
text: You opened it? No alarm? No sweep team? Hah. They really did forget the dead.

speaker: Nova
text: They scratched names into the lid.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Then remember them quiet. Loud gets people recycled. Take the grenades and forget who told you.

speaker: Scrapper
text: You want something useful, cutter girl? There's a cache behind the strip. Old resistance stock.

speaker: Nova
text: Why not grab it yourself?

speaker: Scrapper
text: Because I like breathing. Find the crate, open it clean, and bring me proof it wasn't a trap.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Those grenades are loud history. Use them before history uses you.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Keep your voice down. Dominion buys echoes cheaper than scrap.

speaker: Scrapper
text: Buying, selling, or just looking guilty? Keep it quick either way.

speaker: Doc
text: The coughing stopped. Either you fixed the vents or everyone finally died.

speaker: Nova
text: Fixed. Mostly. Dominion's maintenance request is still doing nothing.

speaker: Doc
text: Then this is faster than paperwork. Corrosive rounds. Ugly medicine for armored problems.

speaker: Doc
text: Don't inhale too deep. The vents are backing poison into my patients again.

speaker: Nova
text: Again?

speaker: Doc
text: Dominion denies repairs faster than I can file them. Patch the cracked vent before the ward turns blue.

speaker: Doc
text: Air's still bad, but now it's regular bad. That's what passes for medicine here.

speaker: Doc
text: If you're bleeding, sit. If you're not, don't start.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: You heading down without shield training? That's how assets become floor paste.

speaker: Nova
text: Shield training?

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: Loading Dock. Riot shield trainer. Bring the loader online, move my cargo, learn Hydraulic Kick, then go play hero in Sector 4.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: You. Cutter girl. Zeke said you're the one who caused that surge in the Pit.

speaker: Nova
text: It was grid instability, Bogs. I just work the cutters.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: Sure it was. Anyway, the interference is overloading the relays in Sector 4. I'm putting you on punishment duty. Descend and clear it.

speaker: Nova
text: Sector 4? That's deep mine. The lift is locked.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: You've got that Mine Access Badge Zeke signed off on. My scanner's updated. Use the elevator, restore power at the Cavern Junction support pillar, then follow the relays until the humming stops.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: You. Cutter girl. Loader's sitting idle and cargo's blocking my schedule.

speaker: Nova
text: There are workers behind that pile.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: Then move it faster. Shipment clock doesn't pause for feelings.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: Route's clear, workers are breathing, and somehow I'm still behind schedule.

speaker: Foreman Bogs
text: Company time ain't charity. Move.

speaker: Miner Bill
text: Bar's quiet if you ignore the vents. I recommend ignoring the vents.

speaker: The Warden
text: Nova. Asset 942-D. Retinal scan flagged for recycling, grid interference logs, and now unauthorized transit.

speaker: Player
text: We're leaving, Warden. Out of our way.

speaker: The Warden
text: Leave? There is nowhere to go. The dome isn't just physical. It's a lock. And I hold the frequency. You are a standing wave that has lost its pitch. I will quiet you myself.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova! You actually did it. The Warden's down. The way to the pod is clear, but security is going to reboot the grid any second.

speaker: Player
text: Let's move. Jed said the Chime is the key.

speaker: Zeke
text: Get inside the pod! I'll secure the hatch. We need to splice that Chime into the console inside.

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova! Connect the Chime to that port on the console. If Jed's math is right, it should establish a resonance that overrides the colony's lock on the pod launchers.

speaker: Player
text: I'm inserting it now. Let's hope the frequency matches.

speaker: Zeke
text: Yes! The core is spooling up. The console's active! We're green for launch. Nova, initiate the sequence on the navigation console!

speaker: Zeke
text: Nova... *cough*... My head feels like a reactor breach. Are you intact?

speaker: Nova
text: I'm fine, Zeke. The pod took the brunt of the impact. How are you holding up?

speaker: Zeke
text: Everything is bruised, but my suit's recycler is still pumping. We're on the surface, Nova. We actually crossed the barrier... but the pod's core is completely fried.

speaker: Zeke
text: Check the core housing in the wreckage. If the Chime is still intact, we need to recover it before this swamp water short-circuits it completely.

speaker: Zeke
text: Check the wreckage of the pod core. We can't leave the Chime behind.

speaker: Zeke
text: The stream of glow-water seems to flow deeper into the concrete structures. Follow it, Nova. I'll catch my breath and catch up.
